There is a reception on the first floor.
There are two elevators near the door.
There are many patients on this floor.
There is not a kitchen on this floor.
Good morning, Graziele. This is a big building!
Yes, it is. Let me show you. There is a reception here on the first floor.
Are there elevators in the building?
Yes, there are two elevators near the door.
And where are the patients?
There are many patients on the fourth floor. There are many rooms for them.
Is there a room for me on this floor?
No, there is not. But there is a nice room on the second floor.

Good morning, and welcome to Morumbi Hospital. The reception is on the first floor, next to the main door. There are two elevators on your right. The cardiology department is on the third floor. There are many rooms for the patients on the fourth floor. For information, please go to the reception. Thank you, and have a good day.

Hi Graziele, this is a quick message about the building. My office is on the second floor, room two ten. There is a small kitchen next to my room. The elevator is near the reception. There are not many people on this floor today, so it is quiet. Come and find me when you arrive. See you soon.

Graziele shows Dr. Carter the hospital. She starts at the reception on the first floor. She points to the two elevators near the door. She shows the rooms for the patients. They go up to the cardiology department on the third floor.
